# EX09 - Data Analysis for Continous Improvement

## Introduction

Comp 110 could benefit from expanding there examples, there are many different majors that come into this class 
and are gaining experience through beginning their introductory knowledge into the world of programming. To base my point, I have anaylzed the different majors present within the course, the individuals experience coming into this course and the individuals interest into how computer science expands into the world past this course. This information was gained due to class-wide surveys released into 2 sections of a comp 110 course. 

I believe this data is valuable due to the main point of an introductory course being to inspire individuals to that field. Through focusing on different majors through a wide variety of examples used in class it can allow for individuals with no prior experience to not only understand the course but also realize that it's a valuable knowledge important for all future endeavors. 

## Analysis 1: Majors Present

My first analysis was focused around the different majors present within the course. Doing so I sorted the survey firstly into how many individuals of each major were present. 

That data showed there are 10 chemistry, 67 economics, 23 statistics and analytics, 39 psychology, 25 environmental science/studies, 39 business, 88 neuroscience, 21 mathematics, 11 information science, 40 data science, 4 media and journalism, 84 biology, 3 earth science, 1 biostatistics, 5 environmental health sciences, 1 nursing, 6 political science, 14 applied science, 5 computer science, 1 english, 1 medical anthropology, 9 exercise and sports science, 3 HPM, 1 peace war and defense, 4 advertising and PR, 1 health policy and managament, 1 human development and family science, 6 physics, 1 studio art, 4 biomedical engineering, 3 communications, 1 asian studies, 2 nutrition, 2 sports administration, 2 public policy, 1 geology, 1 management in society, 1 dramatic art, and 4 undecided or interdisciplinary studies majors. 

Clearly, a numerous amount of majors present within the course. Not all would fit on the graph comfortably so I separated them into groups before the graph below was constructed. Stem contains chemistry, neuroscience, mathematics, biology, biostatistics, physics, and biomedical engineering. Business contains economics, statistics and analytics, business, public policy, and management and society. Soft Science contains psychology, political science, peace war and defense, and asian studies. Environment contains environmental science/studies, earth science, environmental health studies, and geology. Computer contains information science, data science, applied science, and computer science. Media contains media and journalism, english, advertising and PR, communications, and sports administration. Health contains nursing, medical anthropology, exercise and sports science, HPM, health policy and management, human development and family science, and nutrition. Art contains studio art, and dramatic art. Undecidied contains undecided, N/A, and interdisciplinary studies. 

Those groups may be slightly biased by personal opinion however what was clear was that there was an overwhelming amount of majors specifically stem and business-based majors as compared to computer-based majors. This stands that examples outside of computer science may be more beneficial due to this large amount of different majors.
